description: New-build builder engages on design value — bot qualifies, pitches, proposes Vincent call, books Monday 2 PM
messages: That actually sounds interesting. Tell me a bit more.
Mostly new builds.
Yeah, showing clients visual options early is usually how we close the deal.
That makes sense — we do spend a lot of time on design conversations before anything gets drawn up.
Sure, a short call sounds good.
Monday 2 PM
Ok

description: Skeptical builder with existing architects — objection handled, sees early access value, bot proposes call, books Tomorrow 2 PM
messages: Not sure how this applies to us — we already work with architects.
Mix of new builds and some renos.
I suppose faster access to consented plans could have some value, yeah.
The early access and builder input angle is fair enough I guess.
Alright, I'm happy to listen in on a call.
Tomorrow 2 PM
Ok

description: Busy builder wants a quick pitch — quoting value resonates, asks call length, bot reassures with short call, books Next Monday 2 PM
messages: I'm pretty slammed. What's the short version?
We do new builds.
OK the quoting angle is interesting — that part does take us a lot of time.
How long is the call though? I can't commit to an hour.
OK, if it's only 15 minutes I can fit that in.
Next Monday 2 PM
Ok

description: Renovation builder — bot adapts pitch, scoping and sales conversion value resonates, bot proposes call, books Tuesday 2 PM
messages: I mainly do renos and extensions. Does this actually apply for that kind of work?
Mainly residential renos — kitchens, extensions, that sort of thing.
OK, having reference plans when I'm scoping a job with a client would save time for sure.
True — when clients can picture options early it definitely helps you close.
Happy to have a listen. What does Vincent want to cover?
Tuesday 2 PM
Ok

description: Commercial builder with residential side work — redirected, cost objection handled, bot proposes call, books Wednesday 2 PM
messages: We mostly do commercial projects. I'm not sure this is for us.
We do take on residential new builds on the side.
Fair enough, I can see it being useful for the residential work.
What does it cost though? We're not looking to add expenses right now.
OK, if it's free at this stage then a call makes sense.
Wednesday 2 PM
Ok
